name: Muffins Basic and Variations
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease 12 2 ½-inch muffin cups. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Sift together flour, baking powder, sugar, and salt in a medium-sized bowl. Mix well.
3. In a small bowl, beat the egg with a fork. Add milk and oil. Mix until combined.
4. Pour the liquid mixture into the dry ingredients and stir only until the dry ingredients are moistened. The batter will be lumpy.
5. Spoon the batter into prepared muffin cups, filling each about halfway to two-thirds full.
6. Bake for 15 to 20 minutes, or until golden brown. Remove from the pan and serve hot with butter, jam, or marmalade.
Variations:
GINGER MUFFINS: Add ½ cup finely diced candied ginger to the flour mixture before adding liquid.
BANANA PECAN MUFFINS: Prepare the muffin batter but use only ½ cup milk. Add ½ cup chopped pecans and ¼ teaspoon ground nutmeg to the sifted flour. Add 1 cup mashed, peeled banana with the egg, milk, and oil.
BLUEBERRY MUFFINS: Toss 1 cup washed and well-drained fresh or frozen blueberries with the sifted flour mixture before adding liquid.
ORANGE MUFFINS: Cut 2 peeled navel oranges into sections. Place an orange section on top of each muffin before baking. Sprinkle lightly with granulated sugar.
CHEESE MUFFINS: Fold ½ cup grated sharp yellow cheese into the batter with the last few strokes.
SURPRISE MUFFINS: Fill muffin cups about one-third full with batter. Drop ½ teaspoon of your favorite jelly into the center of the batter. Add more batter to fill each cup two-thirds full. Kids love these as a special treat.
COCONUT MUFFINS: Add 1 cup shredded coconut to the batter during the last few strokes. Enjoy with butter and milk for a snack.
CHIVE MUFFINS: Fold ¼ cup chopped chives into the batter during the last few strokes and serve at dinner, perfect with steak and salad.
